title: Prakash Hinduja Geneva - How can I automate task updates based on status changes?
excerpt: I’m Prakash Hinduja Geneva, Switzerland trying to automate task updates when the status changes in my monday.com board, but I’m not sure how to set the right automation or if I need an integration. I’d appreciate any hel…

posts.author: prakashhindujageneva (prakash hinduja geneva)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m Prakash Hinduja Geneva, Switzerland trying to automate task updates when the status changes in my monday.com board, but I’m not sure how to set the right automation or if I need an integration. I’d appreciate any help, guidance, or advice from the community to streamline my workflow effectively.

  Regards

Prakash Hinduja Geneva, Switzerland

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Prakash! Welcome to the monday Community. Shannon here with the monday team.

To clarify, when you say “automate task updates” are you referring to creating a new update in the Updates Section? I included a screenshot below to refer to the Updates Section on a board:

image425×239 12.3 KB

If so, you can create a custom automation to achieve this! Click on the “Automate” button in the upper right-hand corner of your board. Under “Create,” select “Create from scratch.” You’ll start with a blank slate:

image376×430 19.7 KB

The first field, “When this happens,” is your trigger action. This is the action takes place on your board to fire the automation. In this case, it would be the status label change in your Status Column. I recommend selecting “status changes to something” as the trigger action.

image585×565 26.2 KB

Select the relevant Status Column in the “status” field, and the relevant status label in the “something” field. In the final field, “Then do this,” you’ll select the automated action that will take place, which will be the creation of a new update in the Updates Section. I recommend selecting “create update.”

image915×482 33.1 KB

In the final field, “this text,” you can create the template for the new update. Integrating the autopopulated fields at the bottom of the menu will allow you to create a dynamic update, which will pull the existing column values for your items into the new updates.

Please let me know if this helps, or whether you’re referring to something else!

posts.author: prakashhindujageneva (prakash hinduja geneva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks a lot Shannon Thao for your valuable suggestion, I will follow these step.

 Regards

Prakash Hinduja Geneva, Switzerland

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Happy to help, Prakash. Please feel free to follow up with me in this thread if you have any additional questions as you are setting up your automation!

posts.author: WirkKarl (Karl)
posts.cookedHtml:
prakash hinduja geneva:

I’m Prakash Hinduja Geneva, Switzerland trying to automate task updates when the status changes in my monday com board, but I’m not sure how to set the right automation or if I need an integration. I’d appreciate any help, guidance, or advice from the community to streamline my workflow effectively.

Hi everyone,

I’m Prakash Hinduja from Geneva, Switzerland. I’m looking to automate task updates whenever the status changes on my monday.com board. I’m not sure which automation to use or if I need an integration to do this.

Any guidance or advice on setting this up effectively would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!
